resources.string: Fast raw-file mode for huge image folders, SAF fallback for user-picked trees, header-only format sniffing, and bounded worker concurrency.
Use a direct path such as /storage/emulated/0/DCIM to bypass the slow tree picker for very large folders. This is the high-throughput path for overnight jobs.
Fast mode is ready. Direct file-path scanning and sibling writes are enabled.
Fast mode needs All Files Access on Android 11+ so it can bypass the slow system tree picker for huge jobs.
Fast mode is ready with legacy storage permission.
Fast mode needs storage permission on Android 10 and lower.
Grant fast storage access before starting a direct-path folder job.
Fast path is not a folder: %1$s
Pick an image, a SAF folder, or a fast folder path first.
No image or folder selected yet.
Delete originals after a verified smaller output is written
This build uses O(1)-extra-memory streaming scans, not O(1) total-time scans. Fast mode uses raw file paths; SAF mode stays available for provider-backed trees.
Progress will appear here once a job starts.
